title: 1. Client Overview
content:
- **Business name:** Metal Warehouse Inc.
- **Location / niche:** Metal building / steel warehouse supplier in **Sherman, Texas**.
- **Key contacts:**
  - **Tanatsa Kamhunga** — **CEO**, primary documented contact. Communicates by email and appears to review strategy, reports, and implementation requests.
  - A colleague of Tanatsa also emailed about website/menu changes, but the name was **not visible** in the reviewed material.
- **Website:** The exact URL was not explicitly shown in the accessible project context reviewed.
- **GBP / social links:** Not surfaced in the reviewed material.
- **Services / products mentioned:**
  - Metal panels
  - Product list pages
  - Exposed fastening metal panels
  - Concealed fastening metal panels
  - SmartBuild design CTA / building design flow
- **Target areas / locations:** Sherman, Texas is confirmed from Tanatsa’s signature. Wider service geography was not clearly documented in the accessible material.

title: 2. Client Persona & Communication Style
content:
- Communication style is **direct, practical, and operational**.
- Tanatsa appears to care about:
  - Whether the team is already covering important levers
  - Clear progress on deliverables
  - Concrete implementation, not abstract strategy
- The account seems detail-aware when it comes to site structure and product navigation.
- Sensitivities / preferences:
  - Do not introduce outside vendors casually unless they add obvious value.
  - Be prepared to justify why a tool or tactic matters.
  - Keep responses concise and confident.
- Preferred update channel: **email** is clearly evidenced.

title: 3. Timeline of Key Events
content:
- **2025-12-02:** A colleague of Tanatsa emailed about website changes. Requests included:
  - Adding a home-page CTA button linking to **SmartBuild**
  - Reworking the **Panels** menu into a more structured tree with product categories and sub-pages
  - Progress on a **Product List** page was also mentioned
- **2026-02-12:** Internal note/draft indicated Metal Warehouse reports were doing well.
- **Before 2026-02-12:** Metal Warehouse was described as having stronger AI visibility than Augusta Custom Homes in a comparison audit context.
- **2026-02-25:** Tanatsa asked whether **Jumper Media** (a Maps/CTR-style vendor) was already being used. The prepared response was that this angle was already covered internally together with standard SEO fundamentals.

title: 4. SEO / Delivery History
content:
- Work explicitly discussed:
  - Reporting and performance review
  - AI visibility comparison / discussion
  - Website structural work / recommendations:
    - Product list page
    - Home-page CTA linking to SmartBuild
    - Menu / information architecture changes for panels and product hierarchy
  - Internal coverage of Maps CTR / engagement-style tactics
- What appears to have worked:
  - Reports were described as positive
  - Metal Warehouse reportedly outperformed Augusta Custom Homes on AI visibility in SEMrush-based discussion
- What did not surface clearly:
  - Detailed SEO implementation log
  - Specific keyword movements
  - Traffic deltas
  - GBP metrics
  - Link building / citation specifics
- Current state:
  - Positive reporting tone
  - Some site structure / UX items were still active discussion points in the materials reviewed
- Tools / platforms mentioned:
  - **SEMrush**
  - **SmartBuild**
  - Jumper Media was mentioned as an external vendor category, not as the chosen delivery path

title: 5. Writing Style & Tone Used
content:
- Tone used with this client is concise and businesslike.
- Messaging style in drafts avoided jargon-heavy explanations and positioned tactics in plain English.
- Example positioning used in email draft:
  - Jumper was described as a Map Pack vendor that boosts visibility via engagement/CTR-style activity
  - Followed immediately by reassurance that this area was already covered internally
- Preferred angle:
  - Competent, already-on-top-of-it, low-drama execution

title: 6. Ideas Generated (Used or Unused)
content:
- Restructure the product navigation to improve usability and likely SEO crawl clarity
- Add stronger CTA entry into SmartBuild from the home page
- Use AI visibility / AI ranking as a future strategic growth layer
- Keep CTR / engagement-style Maps work handled internally rather than outsourcing it

title: 7. Results & Wins
content:
- Metal Warehouse was described internally as **doing well** in reporting.
- Metal Warehouse was said to have **better AI visibility** than Augusta Custom Homes in a SEMrush comparison.
- No exact keyword positions, traffic numbers, or GBP metrics were visible in the reviewed project material.

title: 8. Open Issues / Unfinished Business
content:
- Confirm whether the requested **Panels menu restructure** and **SmartBuild CTA** were fully implemented.
- Pull exact live SEO / traffic / GBP metrics before any new growth plan is proposed.
- Clarify the current website roadmap around the **Product List** page.
- If AI ranking is being positioned for upsell, package definition and proof assets may still need to be formalized.

title: 9. Anything Else Worth Knowing
content:
- Tanatsa is a key stakeholder and seems to span both strategic and operational concerns.
- This account likely values reliable execution and low-friction communication over long explanations.
- There is enough interest in adjacent tactics (CTR-style Maps work, AI visibility) that proactive recommendations can land well, provided they are framed as controlled, credible additions rather than shiny objects.
